title: Dreamy Smoky Thai Nutella Kanom Ko
description: A winey, flaky, and festive Thai dish featuring Nutella, with a smoky twist, served in a lacquerware bowl, this lacto-vegetarian-friendly dessert is perfect for special occasions.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Mix the flour, sugar, and smoked sea salt in a bowl.
Add the melted butter, eggs, and coconut milk to the bowl and mix until a dough forms.
Roll out the dough to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Cut out small squares of dough, about 3 inches per side.
Place a small spoonful of Nutella in the center of each square.
Fold the dough over the Nutella to form a triangle and press the edges together to seal.
Place the triangles on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper.
Smoke the Thai tea leaves in a small pan over low heat until fragrant.
Place the baking sheet with the triangles over the smoking tea leaves for 1 minute.
Bake the triangles in the preheated oven for 15 minutes, or until golden brown.
